compiled_state.title: As a DSO plugin developer, I can use the using-dso skill name consistently across the codebase
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Rename the using-lockpick skill to using-dso across the entire codebase — directory, files, function names, references, tests, and configs.
**Why**: The using-lockpick name is a legacy artifact that no longer reflects the broader plugin identity (dso). A clean rename establishes consistent naming before new content is added.
**Scope**:
- IN: Rename skill directory (using-lockpick → using-dso), rename inject-using-lockpick.sh → inject-using-dso.sh, rename function hook_inject_using_lockpick → hook_inject_using_dso in session-misc-functions.sh and session-start.sh, update all references in rollback JSON configs (settings-pre-task2/3a/3b/4b.json), update .test-index, rename and update test file (test-using-lockpick-clarification.sh → test-using-dso-clarification.sh), update SKILL.md and HOOK-INJECTION.md frontmatter name field to using-dso
- OUT: CLAUDE.md (contains no using-lockpick references — verified), CHANGELOG.md entries, git commit messages. Sub-agent guidance content (Story 2).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the skill directory exists at plugins/dso/skills/using-dso/ and plugins/dso/skills/using-lockpick/ does not exist
  ← Satisfies: "The skill is named using-dso everywhere"
- When this story is complete, grep -r "using-lockpick" across all .sh, .md, .json, and .conf files returns zero matches (excluding CHANGELOG.md and git commit messages)
  ← Satisfies: "No references to using-lockpick remain in active code, config, skill files, tests, or CLAUDE.md"
- When this story is complete, the test suite passes with the renamed skill and test file
  ← Satisfies: "The renamed startup hook fires successfully in the test suite"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Rename touches 11+ files including tests, rollback configs, and .test-index — ensure all references updated atomically; grep-based verification before closing
- [Reliability] Scenario analysis flagged partial rename as critical deployment risk — verify zero using-lockpick references remain via exhaustive grep
- [Completeness] The standalone hook file plugins/dso/hooks/inject-using-lockpick.sh must be renamed to inject-using-dso.sh. The 4 rollback JSON files reference this filename and must be updated (they are functional configs used during project-setup, not historical snapshots)
- [Completeness] The function hook_inject_using_lockpick() in session-misc-functions.sh and its invocation in session-start.sh must be renamed to hook_inject_using_dso(). A partial rename (updating paths but not function names) will cause a silent failure where the startup hook stops injecting skill context
